data.content: I can see the project — one test will fail (`express` returns `10.00m` but the test expects `15.00m`). Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically on every save.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants hot reload for their test project. In .NET, this is done with `dotnet watch test`. Let me start it up. I can see one test will fail: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` for them.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running (shell: `watch`). It builds and re-runs tests automatically whenever you save a file.

**Current status: 1 passed, 1 failed**
- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expected `15.00` but got `10.00`

The fix is in `CalculateShipping`: the `"express"` case returns `10.00m` instead of `15.00m`. Edit line 23 and save — the watcher will re-run automatically. Want me to fix it?
